parts.title: SKILL 14: LISTEN FOR WISHES
parts.instruction:
TOEFL EXERCISE 14: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question 
on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should remember 
that a wish implies an opposite meaning.

questions.passage:
(man) Do you think we'll be able to get tick ets for the concert?
(woman) I wish there weren't so many people in line in front of us.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The line is short.

reason.en: The woman expresses a wish that there weren't so many people, which implies the opposite is true: there are many people in line.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengungkapkan keinginan agar tidak ada begitu banyak orang, yang menyiratkan kebalikannya yang benar: ada banyak orang dalam antrean.

options.text: There are not very many people in front of them.

reason.en: The woman's wish directly contradicts this statement. She wishes there weren't so many people, implying there are.
reason.id: Keinginan wanita itu secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan ini. Dia berharap tidak ada begitu banyak orang, menyiratkan bahwa ada.

options.text: The line in front of them is too long.

reason.en: The woman states, 'I wish there weren't so many people in line in front of us.' This is a classic example of Skill 14, where a wish implies the opposite of what is stated. Therefore, she means the line is too long.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyatakan, 'Saya berharap tidak ada begitu banyak orang dalam antrean di depan kita.' Ini adalah contoh klasik dari Skill 14, di mana keinginan menyiratkan kebalikan dari apa yang dinyatakan. Oleh karena itu, dia bermaksud bahwa antrean terlalu panjang.

options.text: Not many people want to get tickets to the concert.

reason.en: The woman's statement is about the length of the line, not about the overall demand for concert tickets.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita itu adalah tentang panjang antrean, bukan tentang permintaan keseluruhan untuk tiket konser.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(woman) I'm sorry I didn't tell you about the parking ticket.
(man) I wish you had told me about it.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The woman told him about the ticket.

reason.en: The man says 'I wish you HAD told me,' which implies the opposite is true. Therefore, the woman did NOT tell him.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata 'Saya harap kamu SUDAH memberitahuku,' yang menyiratkan kebalikannya yang benar. Oleh karena itu, wanita itu TIDAK memberitahunya.

options.text: He wanted the woman to get a ticket.

reason.en: The man's wish is about being informed, not about the woman receiving a ticket.
reason.id: Harapan pria itu adalah tentang diberi tahu, bukan tentang wanita itu menerima tiket.

options.text: He was happy to find out about the ticket.

reason.en: The man's statement expresses regret, not happiness, about finding out about the ticket.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu mengungkapkan penyesalan, bukan kebahagiaan, tentang mengetahui tentang tiket tersebut.

options.text: The woman did not tell him about the ticket.

reason.en: The phrase 'I wish you had told me' directly implies that the action (telling him) did not happen. This is a key characteristic of Skill 14, where wishes imply the opposite of reality.
reason.id: Frasa 'Saya harap kamu sudah memberitahuku' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa tindakan (memberitahunya) tidak terjadi. Ini adalah ciri utama dari Skill 14, di mana harapan menyiratkan kebalikan dari kenyataan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(man) Did you see the work schedule for next week?
(woman) I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She is not working too many hours next week.

reason.en: The woman states 'I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then.' This is a wish, and according to Skill 14, a wish implies that the opposite of the wish is true. Therefore, she is implying that she IS working too many hours, not that she is not working too many hours.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja begitu banyak jam saat itu.' Ini adalah sebuah harapan, dan menurut Skill 14, sebuah harapan menyiratkan bahwa kebalikan dari harapan itu benar. Oleh karena itu, dia menyiratkan bahwa dia BEKERJA terlalu banyak jam, bukan bahwa dia tidak bekerja terlalu banyak jam.

options.text: She doesn't have enough hours next week.

reason.en: The woman states 'I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then.' This is a wish, and according to Skill 14, a wish implies that the opposite of the wish is true. Therefore, she is implying that she IS working too many hours, not that she doesn't have enough hours.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja begitu banyak jam saat itu.' Ini adalah sebuah harapan, dan menurut Skill 14, sebuah harapan menyiratkan bahwa kebalikan dari harapan itu benar. Oleh karena itu, dia menyiratkan bahwa dia BEKERJA terlalu banyak jam, bukan bahwa dia tidak memiliki cukup jam.

options.text: She is working too many hours next week.

reason.en: The woman states 'I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then.' This is a wish, and according to Skill 14, a wish implies that the opposite of the wish is true. The opposite of 'I wish I didn't have to work so many hours' is that she does have to work so many hours, meaning she is working too many hours.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja begitu banyak jam saat itu.' Ini adalah sebuah harapan, dan menurut Skill 14, sebuah harapan menyiratkan bahwa kebalikan dari harapan itu benar. Kebalikan dari 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja begitu banyak jam' adalah bahwa dia memang harus bekerja begitu banyak jam, yang berarti dia bekerja terlalu banyak jam.

options.text: She likes working so much.

reason.en: The woman states 'I wish I didn't have to work so many hours then.' This is a wish, and according to Skill 14, a wish implies that the opposite of the wish is true. Her statement is about the number of hours, not about her enjoyment of working.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan 'Saya berharap saya tidak harus bekerja begitu banyak jam saat itu.' Ini adalah sebuah harapan, dan menurut Skill 14, sebuah harapan menyiratkan bahwa kebalikan dari harapan itu benar. Pernyataannya adalah tentang jumlah jam, bukan tentang kesenangannya bekerja.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(man) Are you happy with the changes in the requirements for graduation?
(woman) I wish the department had not made the changes.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The department did not change the requirements.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states 'I wish the department had not made the changes,' which means the opposite of the changes not being made. Therefore, she is not saying the department did not change the requirements.
reason.id: Wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Saya berharap departemen tidak membuat perubahan', yang berarti kebalikan dari perubahan yang tidak dibuat. Oleh karena itu, dia tidak mengatakan departemen tidak mengubah persyaratan.

options.text: She likes the new requirements.

reason.en: The phrase 'I wish...' indicates a desire for something different from the current reality. If she liked the changes, she would not express a wish that they had not been made.
reason.id: Frasa 'Saya berharap...' menunjukkan keinginan untuk sesuatu yang berbeda dari kenyataan saat ini. Jika dia menyukai perubahannya, dia tidak akan mengungkapkan keinginan agar perubahan itu tidak dibuat.

options.text: She changed her apartment just before graduation.

reason.en: The dialogue is about changes in graduation requirements, not about a personal living space. This option is irrelevant to the conversation.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang perubahan persyaratan kelulusan, bukan tentang ruang tamu pribadi. Pilihan ini tidak relevan dengan percakapan.

options.text: She does not like the changes that the department made.

reason.en: According to Skill 14, a wish implies that the opposite of the wish is true. The woman wishes the department had not made the changes, meaning she does not like the changes that were made.
reason.id: Menurut Skill 14, harapan menyiratkan bahwa kebalikan dari harapan itu benar. Wanita itu berharap departemen tidak membuat perubahan, yang berarti dia tidak menyukai perubahan yang dibuat.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you going to the theater with us this weekend?
(man) I wish I had enough money to go.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY.

options.text: He is going to the theater.

reason.en: The man explicitly states 'I wish I had enough money to go,' which implies he does NOT have enough money and therefore cannot go. Option [0] contradicts this.
reason.id: Pria tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Saya berharap saya punya cukup uang untuk pergi,' yang menyiratkan bahwa dia TIDAK punya cukup uang dan oleh karena itu tidak bisa pergi. Pilihan [0] bertentangan dengan ini.

options.text: He doesn't have enough money.

reason.en: The man's statement 'I wish I had enough money to go' directly implies that he does not currently possess enough money to attend the theater. This aligns with Skill 14, which states that a wish implies the opposite of the wish is true.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria tersebut 'Saya berharap saya punya cukup uang untuk pergi' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia saat ini tidak memiliki cukup uang untuk pergi ke teater. Hal ini sejalan dengan Skill 14, yang menyatakan bahwa harapan menyiratkan kebalikan dari harapan itu benar.

options.text: He isn't afraid to go.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention anything about the man's fear of going. His statement is solely about his financial situation.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan apa pun tentang ketakutan pria itu untuk pergi. Pernyataannya semata-mata tentang situasi keuangannya.

options.text: He doesn't want to spend the money.

reason.en: While not having enough money prevents him from going, the primary implication of his wish is the lack of funds, not an unwillingness to spend them if he had them. The wish is about possession, not desire to spend.
reason.id: Meskipun tidak memiliki cukup uang mencegahnya untuk pergi, implikasi utama dari harapannya adalah kurangnya dana, bukan keengganan untuk membelanjakannya jika dia memilikinya. Harapan itu adalah tentang kepemilikan, bukan keinginan untuk membelanjakan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(woman) Did you hear how Harry did on the astronomy exam?
(man) I bet he wishes he had studied harder.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Harry did not prepare enough for the exam.

reason.en: The man's statement 'I bet he wishes he had studied harder' implies that Harry did not study enough, which is the opposite of what he wishes.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'Saya yakin dia berharap dia belajar lebih giat' menyiratkan bahwa Harry tidak belajar cukup, yang merupakan kebalikan dari apa yang dia harapkan.

options.text: Harry studied hard for the exam.

reason.en: This is the opposite of what the man implies. The wish 'he had studied harder' suggests he did not study hard enough.
reason.id: Ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tersirat oleh pria itu. Harapan 'dia belajar lebih giat' menunjukkan bahwa dia tidak belajar cukup giat.

options.text: He has not heard anything about Harry He had a bet with Harry.

reason.en: The man is making a statement about Harry's studying habits, not about his lack of information or a bet.
reason.id: Pria itu membuat pernyataan tentang kebiasaan belajar Harry, bukan tentang kurangnya informasi atau taruhan.

options.text: The algebra course that she is taking is not her favorite.

reason.en: This option is irrelevant to the conversation, which is about Harry's performance on an astronomy exam.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak relevan dengan percakapan, yang membahas tentang kinerja Harry dalam ujian astronomi.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(man) Do you have a good schedule of classes this semester?
(woman) I wish I didn't need to take this alge bra course. I'm not very good at math.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She doesn't need to take the algebra course.

reason.en: The woman says, 'I wish I didn't need to take this algebra course.' This implies that she *does* need to take the course, but she wishes she didn't have to. Therefore, she needs to take it.
reason.id: Wanita itu berkata, 'Saya berharap saya tidak perlu mengambil mata kuliah aljabar ini.' Ini menyiratkan bahwa dia *perlu* mengambil mata kuliah tersebut, tetapi dia berharap tidak harus. Oleh karena itu, dia perlu mengambilnya.

options.text: She has a good schedule of courses this semester.

reason.en: The woman's statement focuses on her dislike for the algebra course, not on her overall schedule. She explicitly states she wishes she didn't need to take it, which contradicts having a good schedule.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita itu berfokus pada ketidaksukaannya terhadap mata kuliah aljabar, bukan pada jadwal keseluruhannya. Dia secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia berharap tidak perlu mengambilnya, yang bertentangan dengan memiliki jadwal yang baik.

options.text: She's good at math, but she's taking the algebra course anyway.

reason.en: The woman states, 'I'm not very good at math.' This directly contradicts the idea that she is good at math.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyatakan, 'Saya tidak pandai matematika.' Ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia pandai matematika.

options.text: He was able to find a cheap apartment.

reason.en: The conversation is about class schedules and math courses. There is no mention of apartments or finances.
reason.id: Percakapan ini tentang jadwal kelas dan mata kuliah matematika. Tidak ada penyebutan tentang apartemen atau keuangan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(woman) Your apartment is in a really great location!
(man) But I wish I had been able to find something cheaper.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: His apartment is too expensive.

reason.en: The man states, 'But I wish I had been able to find something cheaper.' This directly implies that his current apartment is not cheap, meaning it is too expensive.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan, 'Tapi saya berharap saya bisa menemukan sesuatu yang lebih murah.' Ini secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa apartemennya saat ini tidak murah, yang berarti terlalu mahal.

options.text: He doesn't like the apartment's location.

reason.en: The woman's first line praises the apartment's location ('Your apartment is in a really great location!'), and the man's wish is about price, not location. He does not express dissatisfaction with the location.
reason.id: Baris pertama wanita itu memuji lokasi apartemen ('Apartemen Anda berada di lokasi yang sangat bagus!'), dan harapan pria itu adalah tentang harga, bukan lokasi. Dia tidak mengungkapkan ketidakpuasan dengan lokasi tersebut.

options.text: The apartment is cheap because of its location.

reason.en: The man's wish is that he could have found something *cheaper*, which implies the opposite is true: his current apartment is *not* cheap. The location is mentioned positively by the woman, not as a reason for cheapness.
reason.id: Harapan pria itu adalah dia bisa menemukan sesuatu yang *lebih murah*, yang menyiratkan kebalikannya yang benar: apartemennya saat ini *tidak* murah. Lokasi disebutkan secara positif oleh wanita itu, bukan sebagai alasan untuk kemurahan.

options.text: He arrived early at the auditorium.

reason.en: The dialogue is about an apartment and its cost. The phrase 'arrived early at the auditorium' is completely unrelated to the conversation.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang sebuah apartemen dan biayanya. Frasa 'tiba lebih awal di auditorium' sama sekali tidak berhubungan dengan percakapan tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(woman) Why are you sitting all the way in the back of the auditorium?
(man) I wish I hadn't arrived so late. Then I could have gotten a better seat.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: He got one of the best seats in the auditorium.

reason.en: The man states 'I wish I hadn't arrived so late. Then I could have gotten a better seat.' This implies he did NOT get a good seat, making this option incorrect.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan 'Saya berharap saya tidak datang terlambat. Kalau begitu saya bisa mendapatkan tempat duduk yang lebih baik.' Ini menyiratkan dia TIDAK mendapatkan tempat duduk yang bagus, membuat pilihan ini salah.

options.text: He was not early enough to get a seat at the front.

reason.en: The man's wish 'I wish I hadn't arrived so late' directly implies that he arrived late and therefore could not get a better seat, which would likely be at the front.
reason.id: Harapan pria itu 'Saya berharap saya tidak datang terlambat' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia datang terlambat dan oleh karena itu tidak bisa mendapatkan tempat duduk yang lebih baik, yang kemungkinan berada di depan.

options.text: He prefers sitting at the back.

reason.en: The man explicitly states he wishes he had arrived earlier to get a better seat, indicating he does not prefer sitting at the back.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan dia berharap dia datang lebih awal untuk mendapatkan tempat duduk yang lebih baik, menunjukkan bahwa dia tidak lebih suka duduk di belakang.

options.text: He'd like to work on his social skills at the game.

reason.en: The conversation is about seating in an auditorium, not about a game or social skills. This option is irrelevant to the dialogue.
reason.id: Percakapan ini tentang tempat duduk di auditorium, bukan tentang permainan atau keterampilan sosial. Pilihan ini tidak relevan dengan dialog.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 14: Listen for Wishes

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you going to the football game this weekend?
(man) I wish I could, but I have to work on my sociology paper.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He wishes he could work on his term paper for sociology.

reason.en: The man wishes he could go to the game, not that he could work on his paper. The phrase 'work on my sociology paper' is the reason he cannot go, not something he wishes to do instead of going.
reason.id: Pria itu berharap dia bisa pergi ke pertandingan, bukan berharap dia bisa mengerjakan makalahnya. Frasa 'mengerjakan makalah sosiologi saya' adalah alasan dia tidak bisa pergi, bukan sesuatu yang dia ingin lakukan alih-alih pergi.

options.text: He can't attend the game because of his schoolwork.

reason.en: This is a possible inference, but the man explicitly states a wish ('I wish I could') which implies the opposite of what he is saying. The correct answer focuses on the implication of the wish.
reason.id: Ini adalah kesimpulan yang mungkin, tetapi pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan keinginan ('Saya berharap saya bisa') yang menyiratkan kebalikan dari apa yang dia katakan. Jawaban yang benar berfokus pada implikasi dari keinginan tersebut.

options.text: Sociology is less important to him than football this weekend.

reason.en: The man says 'I wish I could' go to the game. According to Skill 14, a wish implies that the opposite of the wish is true. Therefore, he cannot go to the game. The reason given is that he has to work on his sociology paper, which means his schoolwork is preventing him from attending.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata 'Saya berharap saya bisa' pergi ke pertandingan. Menurut Skill 14, keinginan menyiratkan bahwa kebalikan dari keinginan itu benar. Oleh karena itu, dia tidak bisa pergi ke pertandingan. Alasan yang diberikan adalah dia harus mengerjakan makalah sosiologinya, yang berarti pekerjaan sekolahnya mencegahnya untuk hadir.

reason.en: This option is blank and therefore cannot be the correct answer.
reason.id: Opsi ini kosong dan oleh karena itu tidak bisa menjadi jawaban yang benar.

parts.title: SKILL 15: LISTEN FOR UNTRUE CONDITIONS
parts.instruction: In this exercise, listen carefully to each short dialogue and question on the recording, and then choose the best answer to the question. You should be particularly careful of untrue conditions.

questions.passage:
(woman) I'm glad I called and told you about the meeting.
(man) If you had not called me, I would not have known.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The woman did not need to call him.

reason.en: The man explicitly states 'If you had not called me, I would not have known,' which means he *did* need her to call. This option contradicts the statement.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Jika kamu tidak meneleponku, aku tidak akan tahu,' yang berarti dia *membutuhkan* panggilannya. Pilihan ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan tersebut.

options.text: The woman called to let him know about the meeting.

reason.en: The man's statement 'If you had not called me, I would not have known' is a conditional statement implying the opposite is true. The opposite of 'I would not have known' is 'I would have known,' which means he *did* know because she called. This option correctly interprets the meaning of the untrue condition.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'Jika kamu tidak meneleponku, aku tidak akan tahu' adalah pernyataan bersyarat yang menyiratkan kebalikannya benar. Kebalikan dari 'aku tidak akan tahu' adalah 'aku akan tahu,' yang berarti dia *tahu* karena dia menelepon. Pilihan ini dengan benar menafsirkan makna dari kondisi yang tidak benar.

options.text: He's not glad that the woman called.

reason.en: The man says, 'If you had not called me, I would not have known.' This is a polite way of expressing gratitude for her call. He is glad she called because it informed him. This option misinterprets his gratitude.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata, 'Jika kamu tidak meneleponku, aku tidak akan tahu.' Ini adalah cara sopan untuk mengungkapkan terima kasih atas panggilannya. Dia senang dia menelepon karena itu memberinya informasi. Pilihan ini salah menafsirkan rasa syukurnya.

options.text: He already knew about the meeting when the woman called.

reason.en: The man's statement 'If you had not called me, I would not have known' directly implies that he did *not* know before she called. If he had already known, the condition 'If you had not called me' would be irrelevant to his knowledge.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'Jika kamu tidak meneleponku, aku tidak akan tahu' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia *tidak* tahu sebelum dia menelepon. Jika dia sudah tahu, kondisi 'Jika kamu tidak meneleponku' tidak relevan dengan pengetahuannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 15: Listen for Untrue Conditions

questions.passage:
(man) I always seem to get stopped by the police.
(woman) If you drove more slowly, the police would not stop you so often.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man often drives too quickly.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'If you drove more slowly, the police would not stop you so often' implies that the man does NOT drive slowly, and therefore drives too quickly, which is why the police stop him. This is the opposite of the condition stated.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Jika Anda mengemudi lebih lambat, polisi tidak akan terlalu sering menghentikan Anda' menyiratkan bahwa pria itu TIDAK mengemudi lambat, dan oleh karena itu mengemudi terlalu cepat, itulah sebabnya polisi menghentikannya. Ini adalah kebalikan dari kondisi yang dinyatakan.

options.text: The police do not stop the man too much.

reason.en: This contradicts the woman's statement, which implies the police stop the man often because he drives too quickly.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita, yang menyiratkan bahwa polisi sering menghentikan pria itu karena dia mengemudi terlalu cepat.

options.text: The man drove rather slowly.

reason.en: This is the opposite of what the woman implies. The woman implies the man does not drive slowly.
reason.id: Ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tersirat oleh wanita itu. Wanita itu menyiratkan bahwa pria itu tidak mengemudi dengan lambat.

options.text: The police should not stop the man so often.

reason.en: The woman is suggesting a solution to the problem, not stating that the police should not stop him. The implication is that if he drove slower, they wouldn't stop him.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyarankan solusi untuk masalah tersebut, bukan menyatakan bahwa polisi tidak boleh menghentikannya. Implikasinya adalah jika dia mengemudi lebih lambat, mereka tidak akan menghentikannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 15: Listen for Untrue Conditions

questions.passage:
(man) Do you know that some offices are closed next Friday?
(woman) Wouldn't it be nice if we didn't have to work on Friday?
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's so happy they don't have to work on Friday.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'Wouldn't it be nice if we didn't have to work on Friday?' expresses a wish that is the opposite of the reality. The reality is that they *do* have to work on Friday, and she is expressing disappointment about this. Option 0 states she is happy, which is the opposite of what the wish implies.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Bukankah menyenangkan jika kita tidak harus bekerja pada hari Jumat?' mengungkapkan keinginan yang berlawanan dengan kenyataan. Kenyataannya adalah mereka *harus* bekerja pada hari Jumat, dan dia mengungkapkan kekecewaan tentang hal ini.
